title.en: Snare placement, capture, and closure — cold-snare EMR

statement.en: ESGE recommends the best practice approach to snare placement, capture, and closure for cold-snare EMR described in the following list.

text.en: Use an appropriate snare size and type for the lesion.
The tip of the snare catheter (the V of the snare just beyond its tip) should be near the endoscope during closure and constantly visualised.
Maximise tissue capture: use downward pressure and gas aspiration to achieve complete capture of adequate target tissue.
Avoid poor capture or scraping the surface of the polyp; verify mucosal layer is completely excised.

commentary.en: The cold-snare technique is paired across Recs 16 (injection) and 18 (defect inspection) for the cold-snare EMR workflow. Items in GPAT Table 4 referenced as 17(i, ii, vii, viii, ix) cross-map to the hot-snare equivalents in Rec 20 — see GPAT for the assessment view.
